mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS is above its 10-year median.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering immediate risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p < 0.05) in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both above 6-month MA
failure_condition: Joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in >60% of active periods, or interaction term loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during supply-led recovery regimes, or 20-day forward returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO momentum (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls indicate a robust labor market that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: PAYEMS levels predict negative 5-day returns in >65% of observations, or the relationship breaks down with a negative Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: CPIAUCSL values exceeding 260.0 signal entrenched inflationary pressures that force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in >70% of threshold-crossing instances, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions (Mann-Whitney U test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong GDP growth and elevated federal funds rates indicate a policy tightening cycle that actively suppresses economic momentum, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term predicts positive returns in >60% of triggered cases, or the joint condition shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models (F-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, unemployment rates below 4.0% reflect maximum labor market tightness that sustains wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Signal yields negative 5-day returns in >65% of late-cycle observations, or predictive accuracy drops below random chance (accuracy < 50%) when regime filter is applied.
